title: campanula trachelium subsp. urticifolia
intro: Campanula trachelium subsp. urticifolia, also known as nettle-leaved bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Campanula trachelium subsp. urticifolia is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
